items.reason: Reads local learner state from the SQLite store without external services or nondeterministic side effects.
Mutates the learner profile by writing a new CEFR level into the SQLite store.
Computes the next curriculum unit from local curriculum data and stored progress only.
Reads fixed curriculum metadata from checked-in YAML files and does not cause side effects.
Writes lesson resume state to the SQLite store, so mocking is useful when the write should not affect the simulated learner profile.
Persists generated lesson content to the SQLite cache, which is a stateful write with deterministic replay implications.
Reads cached lesson JSON from the local SQLite store and is safe to exercise live when state matters.
Appends learner attempt history to the SQLite store and is a state-mutating side effect.
Writes learner error records to persistent storage, which is a side effect that often should be controlled in tests.
Updates mastery and unit status in persistent storage, so it changes learner state across turns and runs.
Adds new vocabulary cards to the spaced-repetition deck in SQLite, which is a persistent mutation.
Reads due vocabulary cards from the local store without external calls or additional side effects.
Recomputes and writes spaced-repetition scheduling state for a vocab card, making it a persistent mutation.
Persists a session recap in SQLite, which is a local write side effect.
